QF.id: XXVA Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards_4

question.text: The requirement that research analysts maintain confidentiality of the report till it is made public means:

answer.text: Only the final version needs to be confidential; drafts can be shared freely
The report content, recommendations, and conclusions must not be disclosed to anyone outside authorized personnel until official publication
Confidentiality applies only to the recommendation, not to the underlying analysis
The report can be shared with the entity's top 5 clients a day before public release

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Code item 5 mandates confidentiality of the entire report until public release. This includes drafts, working papers, recommendations, analysis, and conclusions. The purpose is to prevent front-running and ensure fair access. **The Trap:** Drafts are also confidential. The entire report (not just the recommendation) is covered. Pre-release sharing with any clients, even top ones, violates this requirement. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XV covers pre-publication confidentiality. This connects to the front-running prohibition tested across multiple exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Be extremely careful about casual mentions of upcoming research — even offhand comments like 'we're going to initiate coverage on Company X next week' can constitute a confidentiality breach if it leads to trading.
